author: @JMWJ-p7m
text:
So....cops dont chase offenders because its 2026 and cops can "fire tracker tags?" and "Most pursuits end in a crash."
So what happens to that offender in the week or so it takes to present a probable cause to a judge and get an arrest warrant for......the car?.....that cops "fired a tracker" into.....which DOES NOT EXIST?

Cops dont arrest cars. Cars dont break laws. (Identifying a car does dick for charging an actual living breathing human being with operating that car or committing a crime). 
So....what happens to the 14 year old girl being trafficked from place to place who was in that car being chased in that week between a violator fleeing and obtaining an arrest warrant for a car? Oh yes, i forgot. We've been over this. There will be no arrest warrant. So, I'm guessing the 14 year old girl is safely delivered to her next workhouse to make lots of money for someone. 
What happens to the 30 lbs of fentanyl in a false floor in that car thats being chased? It finds its way to the streets. 
What happens to the driver, who has warrants for homicide who was driving that car? Well, he kills someone else.

Cops catch bad guys because bad guys are....bad. The idea that those running from police are simply common traffic violators is the thought of a truly ignorant person. 
Ive initiated 22 vehicle pursuits over the years. Been involved in three times as many more than i initiated. 
I can remember one where the driver fled for the observed traffic violation and only the observed violation. ALL of the others were engaged in criminal activity unknown to me at the time i encountered them. 
Ive rescued kidnap victims, stopped drug trafficking, stopped domestic abuse, arrested drunk drivers, caught burglars, arrested people wanted for multiple felonies, arrested people who had stolen the car they were in before the cars owner even knew it was gone. 
A handful of those pursuits ended in crashes where the offender crashed into trees, fences, etc. None ever ended in a crash invilving an innocent third party. Thats one cop in one county in one state. How many cops are there in all the cities and counties of this country? How much crime is solved or prevented after apprehending a violator after running from police?  How many victims are spared by cops doing what cops do? 
No one will ever know, but in my experience, those saved would far exceed those harmed. 

Cops catch bad guys. Its really the only thing bad guys do not like about the criminal justice system. The jail is their social hour. They have no official money. So, they walk with little to no bond. The courts are a joke and they know it. They wont show up to court and when they get a warrant for failing to appear, guess what happens....THEY FLEE! (duh) 
The only thing crooks fear is the being caught part. So, how well does the criminsl justice system work without the "catching them" part? 
It doesn't.
Cops catch bad guys. Thats just the way it work. 
Crooks are responfible for their deeds. Blsming police for tragedies caused by fleeing crimindls is no different than blaming ICE for the two idiots who got ventilated in Minneapolis. The crooks make the decision to resist. 
The harm to a civilian being pinned on the police trying to aprehend that crook is the kind of quick fix, corner cutting, "blame the gun" mentality that only makes bad social problems worse.
